title: rootless: do not add an additional /run to runroot
body:
we are currently using something like /run/user/UID/run as runroot, as
it is already done by Buildah.  This ends up with
/run/user/UID/run/runc for the runc directory.  Change to drop the
additional /run so that runc will use /run/user/UID/runc.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
We will have to add the following patch to Buildah once this PR is merged and we revendor:

```
diff --git a/docs/buildah.md b/docs/buildah.md
index 65d1ca78..c220d4e9 100644
--- a/docs/buildah.md
+++ b/docs/buildah.md
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ Default root dir is configured in /etc/containers/storage.conf
 
 **--runroot** **value**
 
-Storage state dir (default: "/var/run/containers/storage" for UID 0, "/var/run/user/$UID/run" for other users)
+Storage state dir (default: "/var/run/containers/storage" for UID 0, "/var/run/user/$UID" for other users)
 Default state dir is configured in /etc/containers/storage.conf
 
 **--storage-driver** **value**
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Does this break people if they upgrade with running containers? We might want to pop a warning about that somewhere...

Still, LGTM

> Does this break people if they upgrade with running containers? We might want to pop a warning about that somewhere...

yes that will break the storage, saying the configuration is different.  But we already have a recent change for moving from vfs to fuse-overlayfs by default, we are already going to make sad some users at the next upgrade :-)

Fair enough. I'll throw a note in the release notes.

Can we start storing rootless paths in a dedicated libpod.conf in the user's home dir, so upgrades won't be as bad if they've set custom paths?

> Can we start storing rootless paths in a dedicated libpod.conf in the user's home dir, so upgrades won't be as bad if they've set custom paths?

that is already done now, rootless containers use `$HOME/.config/containers/libpod.conf` and `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`
Hm. Can we generate those automatically, when we run as rootless for the first time? They seem like useful things to have around
